edges.description: Severe neuromuscular involvement can manifest as severe muscular hypotonia.
Chronic skeletal-muscle polyglucosan storage can manifest with skeletal muscle atrophy.
Severe fetal or neuromuscular involvement can be accompanied by flexion contractures.
Adult polyglucosan body disease causes corticospinal and peripheral nerve involvement with spastic paraparesis.
Fatal perinatal neuromuscular disease can present in utero with polyhydramnios.
Fatal perinatal neuromuscular disease can present in utero with fetal hydrops.

nodes.description: Supportive care includes hepatology, neurology, cardiology, nutrition, metabolic genetics, functional assessment, imaging, laboratory monitoring, and long-term follow-up tailored to disease severity.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of glycogen storage disease type IV, including adult polyglucosan body disease: A clinical practice resource.
evidence.snippet: best practices for medical management, including (a) imaging of the liver, heart, skeletal muscle, brain, and spine, (b) functional and neuromusculoskeletal assessments, (c) laboratory investigations, (d) liver and heart transplantation, and (e) long-term follow-up care.
